module_mood_contract: Joyful creation — 'it works, on my repo.' Student discovers the compound loop on a real bug from their own backlog; the artifact (CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence) is unambiguously theirs. Any edit that makes M1 feel like a TDD tutorial or a tools-setup module steals the personal-authorship feeling that sets up M2's grounded-competence deepening.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands at 8+ per teaching phase and close.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Cached trace phase mood_score: 8. Close mood_score: 8. No beats below 7. Contract: joyful creation. Two felt moments confirmed: /context number surprise + closed Jira ticket via MCP connector.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve the next module's teaching moment early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:48-49 — Next section frames M2 as plan mode on multi-file work, no resolution of M2's grounded-competence beat. ./CLAUDE.local.md named as carried artifact to M2 context, not as a resolved pedagogy.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Named teaching moment reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Compound step (Ex3: CLAUDE.local.md born from session evidence) is forced inside exercise structure. Push-back moves for rubber-stamping enumerated in maintainer block. /context surprise moment (Ex1) reliably triggers because the unread-slice number is always present. Ticket-close via MCP (Ex3) is the joyful-creation punctuation — lands as long as the connector installs, with tenant-admin fallback path named.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea honors the strategy doc's M1 mood contract and framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea matches strategy doc M1 section precisely: three-phase loop (orient+introspect, fix-tests-first, compound-and-close), same artifacts (./CLAUDE.local.md, PR, ticket closed via connector). Joyful creation mood contract honored throughout module body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: This file's close hands off to M2's open hook.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:48-49 — 'Module 2 is where plan mode earns its keep: multi-file work, a second pass that pressure-tests the plan...' Matches M2's grounded-competence mood. ./CLAUDE.local.md at the top of the next session explicitly named as the M2 context-carry artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers. Demo lectures (ceiling-showing) go at the beginning.
rules_evaluated.evidence: painting-the-picture-with-the-llm.md maintainer block (HEAD, line 38-41) reads: '~3 min opener for Module 1, before the wizard-move demo. Sets the two open questions the training answers by accretion through M1-M6.' Role: opener. The lecture opens with 'The wizard is dead' (ceiling demo — what has changed), poses two open questions the training answers by accretion, and closes with 'Let's go.' This is a ceiling/stance-setting opener, not a meta-frame closer (which would name a pattern the student just lived through). Rule 1 does not fire on opener ceiling-setters. Placement FIRST in module (line 23) matches declared role. Previous stale finding cited the maintainer block declaring 'closer' — that version of the block no longer exists at HEAD. Verified against current file.

M1 is joyful creation, not a contrast-mood module. Rule 2 targets M5-style contrast-mood arcs. One demo/stance lecture (painting-the-picture) + one ceiling demo (the-wizard-move) precede exercises — appropriate dosage for a joyful-creation opener module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:21 — 'The room harvests everyone's before the training adds its own.' Covered by the Connections room-read carve-out (check_pedagogy.md rule 27 + compounded/2026-05-03-pedagogy-connections-room-read-carveout.md). Bare harvest sentence as separate paragraph after Connections question = within carve-out. Maintainer block documents the exception (line 77). No other cohort-management sentences in student-facing body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module file contains no fenced prompt blocks. All forcing functions live in referenced exercise files. Module-level body describes the arc without embedding prose forcing functions. Architectural split is clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block (lines 99-102) contains explicit leap test with three named, falsifiable outcomes: (1) runs /context on a working session — falsifiable by scrollback; (2) writes failing test before fixing — falsifiable by commit history or diff; (3) edits ./CLAUDE.local.md from session evidence with rule quoting specific session beat — falsifiable by rule content. All three use named verbs and artifacts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function documents dominant failure and one recovery move.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block (lines 112-124) carries per-phase failure mode + escape hatch table covering all eight forcing functions across Connections, Ex1, Ex2, Ex3. Each row names failure mode and escape hatch. Format matches rule 47.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honored — does not flatten or pre-resolve.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block (line 71): 'Mood target: joyful creation — it works, on my repo.' Module body stays in joyful-creation register. Bridge teases M2's plan-mode discipline without delivering it. Contract honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea, What You'll Learn bullets, and Key Concepts all lead with positive disciplines: loop shape, /context as tool, self-report as hypothesis, failing test as proof, CLAUDE.local.md as compound artifact, ticket close as first-move-past-repo.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — draft doesn't front-run a downstream teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No Skill use at M1 per strategy doc. Module correctly uses /context (slash command) and MCP (connector). Three-block memory seeded without naming the frame. Plan mode deferred to M2. Klaassen attribution inside Ex3 retro. No downstream moments front-run.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts — each bullet surfaces a theme instance.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts surfaces: compounding (loop shape + CLAUDE.local.md), 90% correct (agent self-report as hypothesis, unread slice), self-awareness (verify against artifact), capture-your-world (rules born from evidence). At least three themes surfaced.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — gates §§3/4/5 run before decorating; breadcrumbs on survivors only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Gates §3, §4, §5 all PASS. Three of six bullets carry **Remember:** breadcrumbs in engineer register. No standalone themes section. Shape clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn — capability register, not syllabus. Strategic theme verbs lead.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bullets lead with Run, Introspect, Fix, Compound — strategic theme verbs. Engineer-peer register. Concrete verb + artifact. No Bloom-tag parentheticals, no didactic stamps, no apparatus tails. Trailing fragment 'The first move outside the repo' is carve-out (a): outcome-side description in engineer register.
